title: Stale forwarded connection can be reaped while still in use
body: In Server::handle_connection, accepted TCP streams are inserted into the conns map and a background task removes them after a hardcoded 10 second sleep if the client hasn't sent Accept yet. On a slow client this silently drops a legitimate connection instead of giving any feedback. Consider making the timeout configurable or logging which remote addr was dropped.

title: No rate limiting on authentication challenge attempts
body: Authenticator::server_handshake validates a single HMAC-SHA256 response per connection, but nothing stops a client from opening thousands of TCP connections per second to brute-force the secret offline. Might be worth adding basic backoff or connection throttling per IP in server.rs.

title: bind-tunnels defaults silently to bind-addr with no way to verify
body: When --bind-tunnels is omitted, Command::Server falls back to bind_addr via bind_tunnels.unwrap_or(bind_addr) in main.rs, but there's no log line stating which address tunnels actually bound to, which makes debugging NAT/firewall setups confusing.

title: Client does not reconnect after control connection drops
body: Client::listen returns Ok(()) as soon as conn.recv() yields None (server closed the socket), and main.rs just lets the process exit. For long-running tunnels it would be much more useful if the client retried connecting to `to`/CONTROL_PORT with backoff instead of dying.

title: Document the null-delimited JSON framing protocol
body: Delimited in shared.rs uses AnyDelimiterCodec with a null byte delimiter and a 256 byte MAX_FRAME_LENGTH, but there's no doc comment explaining why 256 was picked or what happens if a ClientMessage/ServerMessage serializes to something larger. Would help third parties reimplementing the protocol.
